title: How Cold Heading Technology Improves Fastener Strength
excerpt: The manufacturing process of industrial fasteners has evolved significantly. Cold heading, a method of forming a fastener by punching it at room temperature, has become the industry standard for high-volume, high-strength production.
content:
The manufacturing process of industrial fasteners has evolved significantly. Cold heading, a method of forming a fastener by punching it at room temperature, has become the industry standard for high-volume, high-strength production.

Why Cold Heading?

Unlike traditional machining, which cuts away material and disrupts the metal's grain structure, cold heading compacts the grain. This results in a much higher tensile strength and fatigue resistance.

Precision Control

At Hangfan, our automated lines ensure each fastener is identical to within 0.01mm. This precision is critical for the automotive and aerospace sectors, where even the smallest variance can lead to mechanical failure.
author: Tech Team

title: New Cold Heading Production Line Installed
